source_paper_title: On the Smoothed Complexity of Combinatorial Local Search
context:
Page 33, Section 6 (Conclusion and problems)

"In this paper, we show that the problem of computing (pure, exact) equilibria in congestion games admits a smoothed polynomial running time algorithm for special classes of general and network congestion games (see Theorem 4.2 and Theorem 4.8, respectively). However, outside of these restricted instances of the problem, the complexity remains unresolved."
title: Smoothed complexity of computing exact pure Nash equilibria in unrestricted congestion games
latex:
Given a finite congestion game with player set \(N=[n]\), resource set \(R\), and strategy sets \(\Sigma_i\subseteq 2^R\) for each player \(i\in N\), let a strategy profile be \(\sigma=(\sigma_1,\dots,\sigma_n)\in\Sigma:=\prod_{i\in N}\Sigma_i\). For each resource \(r\in R\), the load under \(\sigma\) is \(\ell_r(\sigma):=|\{i\in N: r\in \sigma_i\}|\). Each resource has a latency function \(\kappa_r:[n]\to\mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}\), and player \(i\)'s cost under \(\sigma\) is \(C_i(\sigma):=\sum_{r\in \sigma_i}\kappa_r(\ell_r(\sigma))\). A profile \(\sigma^*\) is a (pure, exact) Nash equilibrium (PNE) if for all \(i\in N\) and all \(\sigma'_i\in\Sigma_i\), \[C_i(\sigma^*)\le C_i(\sigma'_i,\sigma^*_{-i}).\]

Consider the following smoothed-input model: the numerical parameters defining the latencies are independently perturbed by continuous distributions with densities bounded by \(\phi\).
- General latencies: for each \(r\in R\) and \(\ell\in[n]\), \(\kappa_r(\ell)\) is drawn independently from a distribution on \([0,1]\) with density at most \(\phi\).
- Polynomial latencies of maximum degree \(d\): for each \(r\in R\) and coefficient index \(j\in\{0,1,\dots,d_r\}\) with \(d_r\le d\), the coefficient \(\alpha_{r,j}\) in \(\kappa_r(\ell)=\sum_{j=0}^{d_r}\alpha_{r,j}\ell^j\) is drawn independently from a distribution on \([0,1]\) with density at most \(\phi\).
- Step-function latencies with breakpoints \(B_r\subseteq[n]\): for each \(r\in R\) and \(j\in B_r\), the jump size \(\alpha_{r,j}\) in \(\kappa_r(\ell)=\sum_{j\in B_r\cap[\ell]}\alpha_{r,j}\) is drawn independently from a distribution on \([0,1]\) with density at most \(\phi\).

Problem: Determine whether there exists an algorithm that, for every such smoothed congestion game instance (without additional restrictions such as \(B\)-restrained deviations or \((A,B)\)-compactness), computes an exact PNE in expected time polynomial in the input size and \(\phi\). Equivalently, resolve the smoothed computational complexity (polynomial/quasipolynomial/superpolynomial) of finding an exact PNE in general (unrestricted) congestion games under these smoothed models.
open_problem_quote: "In this paper, we show that the problem of computing (pure, exact) equilibria in congestion games admits a smoothed polynomial running time algorithm for special classes of general and network congestion games (see Theorem 4.2 and Theorem 4.8, respectively). However, outside of these restricted instances of the problem, the complexity remains unresolved."
